name: serialize_parse_roundtrip_external_body
reason: `theorem_serialize_parse_roundtrip` is `external_body` — the fundamental serialize-then-parse roundtrip property is trusted without proof, so any implementation of `SecureSpecCombinator` gets this for free even if its `spec_serialize`/`spec_parse` are inconsistent

name: wf_default_always_true
reason: The default `wf` returns `true` for all values — any combinator that doesn't override `wf` accepts all values as well-formed, which weakens the roundtrip guarantee to hold vacuously for malformed data

name: requires_default_always_true
reason: The default `requires` returns `true` unconditionally — any combinator that doesn't override it has no precondition, so all lemmas guarded by `self.requires()` fire without constraint

name: serialize_no_injectivity
reason: The roundtrip axiom implies serialize is injective on well-formed values — this is a consequence of the external_body axiom that should be an explicit theorem, not a side-effect of a trusted assumption

name: parse_length_matches_serialize_length
reason: The roundtrip axiom forces parse to consume exactly the entire serialized output — there is no room for a format where parsing legitimately consumes fewer bytes than were serialized, which may over-constrain implementations with trailing data
